title: Reshape brief_dev to ADR-012: facts in the workflow, sealed reports through it, rendering on the worker
blocked_by: RM-022 (aion_kit: the opaque payload seal/raw/peek primitive, the aion_flow pass-through type, and the template/json primitives this brief consumes) must land first
purpose: Makes the deterministic workflow process thin (ADR-012). brief_dev currently decodes full ScoutReport/DevReport/ReviewReport and renders every stage prompt inside workflow code — heavy work re-executed on every replay (engine invariant 2) and the exact regression that crashed a real dogfood run with a silent heap-full. This brief reshapes the inner data flow: the four agentic activities return a small facts projection PLUS the full report sealed as an opaque aion_flow payload; the workflow threads sealed payloads and reads only facts; prompt rendering and report decoding move into the activity bodies on the worker (built on aion_kit's template + payload primitives). The agents' output is unchanged (ADR-012 quote, CN5) and the outer stacked-dev arc stays byte-immutable (CN1, ADR-008) — only where decoding and rendering happen moves. After the reshape the TK-002 dogfood must still run end to end through real norn (CN9).
task: Reshape examples/stacked-dev so brief_dev.gleam holds facts, not reports, and renders no prompts. SCOPE IN: the facts types and codecs (facts.gleam), the sealed-payload activity result reshape (activities.gleam, types.gleam, codecs_core.gleam, codecs_workflows.gleam), the worker-side render path (render.gleam + worker/src/render.rs built on the aion_kit template primitive), the agentic locals/handlers reshaped to render-and-seal and the enrich_brief activity reshaped to decode the sealed payloads, the brief_dev.gleam reshape itself, the brief_dev_output.json schema update + codegen regen, the two outer-arc consumption sites in stacked_dev.gleam, and the deletion of prompts.gleam with its five projection test modules (their pins move to render_test.gleam). SCOPE OUT: lifting the yg/cargo/git harness into a reusable module (WT-002) and lifting the control skeleton into a parameterised pipeline (WT-003) — this brief reshapes brief_dev in place; the harness locals (provision/warm/scoped/full/land) and the brief_dev control flow stay where they are, only their data shapes adjust. DEPENDENCY: blocked on RM-022 — this brief uses aion_kit's opaque payload (seal to wrap a report, raw to unwrap it worker-side) and the aion_flow pass-through payload type a workflow can hold without decoding, plus aion_kit's template primitive for the render path; add the aion_kit dependency to gleam.toml and the Rust aion-kit crate to worker/Cargo.toml as part of R1. THE FACTS RULE (CN4, P3): the facts a type carries are exactly the values the pre-reshape workflow read off a full report — DevFacts.blocked_requirement_ids (was dev_report.enrichments[].status == blocked), DevFacts.changed_files (was the deduplicated files_changed[].path), ReviewFacts.drifted (was enrichments[].alignment == drifted, with issues), ReviewFacts.has_fixes (was any enrichment fixes non-empty); scout produced no routing fact beyond success, so ScoutFacts is a success marker. No new routing signal is invented and none is dropped — the reviewer checks each fact field against the brief_dev.gleam control flow that read it. OUTER-ARC NOTE (CN1): stacked_dev.gleam derives its DevResult from brief_dev_result.dev today by reading a full DevReport (changed_files + summary); after the reshape it reads the carried facts (changed_files) and the carried dev summary fact instead, and threads the sealed reports straight to enrich_brief — the provision/gate/review/land/signal/spawn sites are not touched. CONTROL-FLOW NOTE (C13): the stage flow, the seven status phases, the bounded verify-fix loop with required verify_fix_cap/round_backoff_ms, and the six-variant BriefDevError are preserved exactly as RM-017 landed them; this brief changes the data the loop carries, not the loop.

requirements.title: aion_kit dependency and the facts types with codecs
requirements.spec: Structural. Add aion_kit to examples/stacked-dev/gleam.toml and the aion-kit Rust crate to examples/stacked-dev/worker/Cargo.toml (the package and worker consume RM-022's payload + template surface). Create src/stacked_dev/facts.gleam defining: DevFacts(blocked_requirement_ids: List(String), changed_files: List(String), summary: String); ReviewFacts(drifted: List(DriftedRequirement), has_fixes: Bool); ScoutFacts(ok: Bool). Each gets a hand-written codec that round-trips losslessly with deterministic encoding. DriftedRequirement is reused from types.gleam (id, issues) — do not redefine it. WHEN a facts codec encodes a value, THE SYSTEM SHALL emit the same canonical key order on every call (replay-stable), and SHALL NOT include any field of the full stage report beyond the projected facts. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT add a facts field that the pre-reshape brief_dev did not read off a report (CN4): the summary on DevFacts is the one value the outer arc's DevResult derivation reads, carried as a fact precisely so the workflow needs no full report.
acceptance: src/stacked_dev/facts.gleam defines exactly DevFacts, ReviewFacts, ScoutFacts with the fields named in the spec and no others.

DevFacts.changed_files and DevFacts.blocked_requirement_ids correspond to the changed_files(dev_report) dedup and the enrichments[].status == blocked filter brief_dev.gleam performs today (the reviewer diffs the old workflow logic against the new fact derivation).
Each facts codec round-trips a constructed value to an equal value, and re-encoding the decoded value yields a byte-identical string (deterministic key order).
examples/stacked-dev/gleam.toml lists aion_kit and examples/stacked-dev/worker/Cargo.toml lists the aion-kit crate.
grep for ScoutReport, DevReport, or ReviewReport in src/stacked_dev/facts.gleam returns no matches — the facts module references no full-report type.

requirements.title: Sealed-payload activity results and their codecs
requirements.spec: Reshape the four agentic activities so each returns its facts paired with the full report sealed as an opaque aion_flow pass-through payload. In src/stacked_dev/types.gleam: add ScoutOutcome(facts: ScoutFacts, report: SealedPayload), DevOutcome(facts: DevFacts, report: SealedPayload), ReviewOutcome(facts: ReviewFacts, report: SealedPayload) where SealedPayload is aion_kit's opaque pass-through type; reshape StartupResult's Developed variant to Developed(dev_outcome: DevOutcome). In src/stacked_dev/activities.gleam: scout returns ScoutOutcome, dev_resume returns DevOutcome, dev_review returns ReviewOutcome, and dev (through the startup envelope) returns Developed(DevOutcome) — each binds the new outcome codec, NOT the generated full-report json_codec. In src/stacked_dev/codecs_core.gleam: add scout_outcome_codec, dev_outcome_codec, review_outcome_codec composing the facts codecs with aion_kit's payload codec; update startup_result_codec for the reshaped Developed. WHEN an outcome codec encodes, THE SYSTEM SHALL carry the sealed report as the opaque payload bytes without decoding them, and WHEN it decodes, THE SYSTEM SHALL leave the payload sealed (no raw/peek). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT bind the generated scout_report/dev_report/review_report json_codec as any activity's output codec anymore — the report rides sealed. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT alter the codecs or names of provision_workspace, warm_build, scoped_checks, full_checks, request_review, land, or assemble_wave (CN1).
acceptance: scout's output type is ScoutOutcome, dev_resume's is DevOutcome, dev_review's is ReviewOutcome, and StartupResult.Developed carries a DevOutcome.

grep shows no activity definition in activities.gleam binding codec.json_codec over stage_io.scout_report_decoder/dev_report_decoder/review_report_decoder — the generated report json_codecs are no longer activity output codecs.

scout_outcome_codec, dev_outcome_codec, and review_outcome_codec round-trip a constructed outcome (facts + a sealed payload built from a schema-valid report) to an equal value with the payload still sealed.

The eight non-agentic activity name constants and their codec bindings (provision_workspace, warm_build, scoped_checks, full_checks, request_review, land, enrich_brief, assemble_wave) are byte-identical to their pre-brief values.

requirements.title: Worker-side render path on the aion_kit template primitive
requirements.spec: Move prompt rendering out of the workflow and into the worker. Create src/stacked_dev/render.gleam exposing scout_render, dev_render, review_render, and resume_render — pure functions of the brief document, resolved context, and the decoded prior-stage report(s), built on aion_kit's template primitive rather than re-implemented string assembly. Create worker/src/render.rs as the Rust mirror for the deployed worker. THE SYSTEM SHALL preserve the projection discipline of the retired prompts.gleam (C10): anchored ADRs as 'id: title — decision' lines, verbatim quotes with speaker attribution, requirements with inline resolved C#/S# texts, boundaries in every stage prompt; the dev render inlines scout findings per requirement; the review render renders the dev attestation and the measured check result as two labelled sections but NEVER the scout (ADR-010). render.gleam SHALL NOT import aion/workflow, aion/activity, aion/query, aion/signal, or any CLI/locals module — it is pure, called only from locals on the worker. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT leave a second rendering path in workflow code: rendering exists only here and in worker/src/render.rs.
acceptance: render.gleam exposes scout_render, dev_render, review_render, resume_render as pure functions; grep -E 'import (aion/(workflow|activity|query|signal)|stacked_dev/(cli|locals|activities))' src/stacked_dev/render.gleam returns no matches.
review_render renders a labelled dev-attestation section and a labelled measured-checks section and does not render any scout block (ADR-010): grep confirms no scout-block call in review_render.
dev_render inlines the scout finding for each requirement (the fixture's R1 scout approach sentinel appears within the R1 section).
render.gleam composes prompts via the aion_kit template primitive (grep shows the aion_kit template import and usage), not bespoke concatenation duplicating prompts.gleam.
worker/src/render.rs exists and exposes the same four render entrypoints the handlers call.

requirements.title: Agentic locals render-and-seal; enrich_brief decodes the sealed payloads
requirements.spec: In src/stacked_dev/locals.gleam reshape the four agentic locals. WHEN locals.scout / locals.dev / locals.dev_review / locals.dev_resume run, THE SYSTEM SHALL build the stage prompt by calling the matching render.gleam function (from the brief document, resolved context, and the decoded prior-stage payload(s) the input now carries), invoke norn exactly as before (same argv, same deterministic session ids '<branch>-scout' / '<branch>' / '<branch>-review', CN1), decode the full report from norn stdout (the existing require_report bare-or-{output} seam), project the facts from it, seal the full report with aion_kit, and return the (facts, sealed payload) outcome. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT accept a pre-rendered prompt string on the activity input anymore — the input carries the document/context/prior payload and the local renders (the rendering crossed from workflow to worker). Reshape locals.enrich_brief: it now receives the sealed scout/dev/review payloads, unwraps each with aion_kit payload.raw + the report decoder, and merges the decoded full reports into the brief exactly as it does today (the CN3 authored-divergence guard and the append-only merge are unchanged). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT decode a full report anywhere outside enrich_brief and the facts-projection step inside the four agentic locals — no workflow module decodes a report (CN2). worker/src/handlers.rs mirrors every reshaped local invocation-for-invocation (render, seal, project facts; enrich_brief unwraps).
acceptance: Each agentic local builds its prompt via the corresponding render.gleam function (grep shows render.scout_render etc. called in locals), and no agentic local takes a pre-rendered prompt: ScoutInput/ReviewInput/DevInput/ResumeInput carry the document/context/prior payloads, not a prompt string.

The norn argv and session ids in the four agentic locals are byte-identical to their pre-brief values (--print, --session-id <branch>[-scout|-review] / --resume, --workspace-root, --output-schema, --output-format json) — the reviewer diffs the argv lists.
Each agentic local seals the decoded full report and returns the facts projected from it; enrich_brief unwraps the sealed payloads with aion_kit payload.raw and merges the decoded reports.

grep for stage_io report decoders across all src/ modules shows them used only inside the four agentic locals (facts projection) and locals.enrich_brief (merge) — no workflow module (brief_dev.gleam, stacked_dev.gleam) decodes a report.
worker/src/handlers.rs mirrors the reshaped scout/dev/dev_review/dev_resume/enrich_brief handlers (render-and-seal; unwrap-and-merge).

requirements.title: Reshape brief_dev.gleam to thread sealed payloads and read only facts
requirements.spec: Reshape src/brief_dev.gleam (ADR-012, CN2). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT import stacked_dev/prompts (deleted) or stacked_dev/render (worker-side), and SHALL NOT decode or field-access any ScoutReport, DevReport, or ReviewReport. WHEN scout returns, THE SYSTEM SHALL hold its ScoutOutcome (facts + sealed report) and route only on ScoutFacts. WHEN the startup fan-out returns, THE SYSTEM SHALL read DevFacts.blocked_requirement_ids to fail DevBlocked, and DevFacts.changed_files to drive scoped_checks — never a decoded report. WHILE verifying, THE SYSTEM SHALL run the bounded verify-fix loop exactly as today (scoped_checks over DevFacts.changed_files; on CheckFail, round >= verify_fix_cap fails VerifyFixExhausted, else a durable round_backoff_ms sleep then dev_resume, recursing with round+1) — the loop, the seven status phases (scouting, developing, verifying, fixing, reviewing, hardening, converged), and the six BriefDevError variants are unchanged (C13). WHEN dev_review returns, THE SYSTEM SHALL read ReviewFacts.drifted to fail ReviewDrifted (evaluated BEFORE the harden re-verify) and ReviewFacts.has_fixes to decide the harden re-run; on harden CheckFail it fails HardenRegressed. WHEN converged, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the reshaped BriefDevResult carrying the three sealed payloads and the facts the outer arc needs. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT dispatch any activity other than scout, warm_build, dev, scoped_checks, dev_resume, dev_review, and SHALL NOT impose any cap/backoff/deadline beyond the two required inputs.
acceptance: grep -E 'import .*stacked_dev/(prompts|render)' examples/stacked-dev/src/brief_dev.gleam returns no matches.

grep for .enrichments, scout_report_decoder, dev_report_decoder, review_report_decoder, or any ScoutReport/DevReport/ReviewReport field access in brief_dev.gleam returns no matches — the workflow reads facts only (CN2, C5).
The drift check (ReviewFacts.drifted -> ReviewDrifted) is evaluated before the has_fixes harden re-verify, preserving the pre-reshape ordering.

grep -o 'set_status("[a-z]*"' brief_dev.gleam yields exactly scouting, developing, verifying, fixing, reviewing, hardening, converged; workflow.sleep appears exactly once with duration.milliseconds(input.round_backoff_ms).
The only activities.* calls in brief_dev.gleam are scout, warm_build, dev, scoped_checks, dev_resume, dev_review.

requirements.title: Reshape BriefDevResult, the output schema, and regenerate the IO module
requirements.spec: Reshape the brief_dev result to carry sealed reports plus facts. In src/stacked_dev/types.gleam: BriefDevResult becomes BriefDevResult(scout: SealedPayload, dev: SealedPayload, review: SealedPayload, dev_facts: DevFacts, review_facts: ReviewFacts, verify_rounds: Int, build_warm: BuildWarm) — the three sealed payloads feed enrich_brief, the facts feed the outer arc's DevResult derivation, so the parent never decodes a report (C6, C17). In src/stacked_dev/codecs_workflows.gleam: update brief_dev_result_codec to compose the aion_kit payload codec for the three sealed reports with the facts codecs and the two scalars; brief_dev_input_codec is unchanged. Update schemas/brief_dev_output.json to the reshaped result inside the codegen v1 subset (no $ref/$defs/oneOf/anyOf/allOf/const/default/type-unions; additionalProperties false throughout) — the three sealed-report fields are opaque payload objects per aion_kit's payload schema shape, the facts fields mirror the facts types. Re-run aion codegen so src/aion_stacked_dev_io.gleam reflects the new output schema; the regenerated module SHALL NOT be hand-edited. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT change schemas/brief_dev_input.json (the input is unchanged).
acceptance: BriefDevResult has exactly the fields scout, dev, review, dev_facts, review_facts, verify_rounds, build_warm; scout/dev/review are SealedPayload, not full report types.
brief_dev_result_codec round-trips a constructed BriefDevResult (sealed payloads built from schema-valid reports + facts) to an equal value with payloads still sealed.

python3 -c "import json; s=json.load(open('examples/stacked-dev/schemas/brief_dev_output.json')); assert s['additionalProperties'] is False and sorted(s['required'])==sorted(['scout','dev','review','dev_facts','review_facts','verify_rounds','build_warm'])" exits 0.
grep -F '"$ref"' and grep -F '"oneOf"' over examples/stacked-dev/schemas/*.json produce no matches.
cargo run -p aion-cli -- codegen examples/stacked-dev --check exits 0 and src/aion_stacked_dev_io.gleam reflects the reshaped output schema.

requirements.title: Adjust the two outer-arc consumption sites without touching the contracts
requirements.spec: In src/stacked_dev.gleam adjust ONLY the two sites that consume the brief_dev child result (CN1, C16, C17). The dev_result_of derivation SHALL build the outer DevResult (session_id, files_touched, summary) from the reshaped BriefDevResult's carried facts — files_touched from dev_facts.changed_files, summary from dev_facts.summary — and SHALL NOT decode a full DevReport. enrich_then_land SHALL pass the three carried sealed payloads (scout, dev, review) straight to the enrich_brief activity, which unwraps them worker-side (R4). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT change provision_workspace, the gate child spawn, request_review, the review_verdict signal/receive, land, the StartupTask/StartupResult envelope, or the brief_dev spawn_and_wait shape — those sites are byte-identical pre- and post-brief (CN1). IF the execution block's attestation is needed, THE SYSTEM SHALL source it from the sealed dev report via the enrich_brief worker path, not by decoding the report in the workflow.
acceptance: dev_result_of reads dev_facts.changed_files and dev_facts.summary; grep shows no DevReport field access (.enrichments, .summary on a report, changed_files(dev_report)) in stacked_dev.gleam.
enrich_then_land threads the three sealed payloads to enrich_brief; the workflow decodes no report.
The provision/gate-spawn/request_review/review-signal/land/startup-envelope/brief_dev-spawn sites in stacked_dev.gleam are byte-identical to their pre-brief text (the reviewer diffs each block).
The review_signal_name, status_query_name, and the workflow.spawn_and_wait(brief_dev.workflow_type, ...) call are unchanged.

requirements.title: Delete prompts.gleam, move the pins to render_test, add facts/sealed-payload tests
requirements.spec: Delete src/stacked_dev/prompts.gleam and its five projection test modules (prompts_render_test, prompts_scout_test, prompts_dev_test, prompts_review_test, prompts_resume_test) — the in-workflow projection is retired, no second rendering path remains (ADR-002, C9). Create test/render_test.gleam carrying the moved budget-and-content pins against the seeded fixture (docs/design/brief-dev/briefs/BD-001.json via the existing prompt_fixture support): scout render <= 6000, dev render <= 9000, review render <= 12000 characters, the ADR/quote/boundary/requirement content assertions, and the ADR-010 no-scout-in-review assertion. Create test/facts_codecs_test.gleam: round-trip and wire-pin tests for the facts codecs (DevFacts, ReviewFacts, ScoutFacts) and the three outcome codecs and brief_dev_result_codec (sealed payloads stay sealed across the round-trip). Update test/aion_stacked_dev_test.gleam and test/support/shims.gleam for the facts/sealed-payload flow: the shims emit the report the worker seals, and the hermetic stage-outcome assertions read facts where they previously read decoded reports — the stage outcomes (scout failure, dev block, verify exhaustion, review drift, harden regression, happy path) are unchanged in meaning (C13). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT invent report shapes outside the stage-contract schemas.
acceptance: src/stacked_dev/prompts.gleam and the five prompts_*_test.gleam modules do not exist; grep -rl 'stacked_dev/prompts' examples/stacked-dev/src examples/stacked-dev/test returns nothing.
test/render_test.gleam exists and contains the budget pins 6000, 9000, 12000 and the ADR-010 assertion that the review render contains no scout block.
test/facts_codecs_test.gleam round-trips each facts type and each outcome codec; a BriefDevResult round-trips with its three payloads still sealed.
cd examples/stacked-dev && gleam test runs render_test, facts_codecs_test, and the updated aion_stacked_dev_test and all pass.

The hermetic suite still covers scout failure, dev block, verify exhaustion, review drift unfixed, review fixes with passing re-verify, harden regression, and the happy path — the same stage outcomes as before the reshape.

requirements.title: Wire-compat pins and the re-dogfood through real norn
requirements.spec: Update examples/stacked-dev/worker/tests/wire_compat.rs to pin every facts type and every sealed-payload outcome envelope (ScoutOutcome, DevOutcome, ReviewOutcome, the reshaped Developed, the reshaped BriefDevResult) byte-compatible BOTH directions against the Gleam codecs; update worker/tests/handlers_shims.rs for the render-and-seal handler shape. Then re-run the TK-002 dogfood through real norn against the aion repo (CN9, S5): deploy all four archives, dispatch the brief, and assert the pipeline scouts/develops/verifies/reviews/hardens and lands on main with the enriched brief in the merge — with no workflow-process heap exhaustion. WHILE the run is in review, THE SYSTEM SHALL survive a SIGKILL of the server and resume without re-executing completed activities (S6). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT mark this brief complete on a green type-check alone — the dogfood landing is the proof the reshape works (the run is the verification, mirroring brief-dev's all-norn goal).
acceptance: wire_compat.rs pins DevFacts, ReviewFacts, ScoutFacts, ScoutOutcome, DevOutcome, ReviewOutcome, and the reshaped BriefDevResult byte-identical both directions; cargo test in worker/ passes.

The TK-002 dogfood run lands on main: the merged commit contains the enriched brief file (per-R# scout/dev/review blocks and the execution block), and the server logs show no heap-exhaustion crash in the workflow process.
A SIGKILL of the server mid-review, followed by restart and the review_verdict signal, resumes the run to land with zero re-executed activities (verified against the event history).
The dogfood's enriched brief validates against brief.schema.json (python3 validate.py on the landed brief).

boundaries: SHALL NOT lift the yg/cargo/git harness (provision/warm/scoped/full/land) into a reusable module — that is WT-002; this brief reshapes their data shapes in place only where the facts/sealed-payload flow requires, leaving their CLI invocations byte-unchanged.
SHALL NOT lift the brief_dev control skeleton into a parameterised pipeline module — that is WT-003; brief_dev.gleam stays a concrete workflow body in this brief.

SHALL NOT alter any OUTER stacked-dev contract — provision/gate/review/land argv and sequencing, the StartupTask/StartupResult envelope, the review_verdict signal, or the brief_dev spawn_and_wait shape (CN1, ADR-008). The only stacked_dev.gleam edits are the two child-result consumption sites (R7).

SHALL NOT change the stage-contract schemas (scout/dev/review report) or any file under docs/design-system/ — the agents' output is unchanged (CN5, ADR-012 quote); only where reports are decoded moves. The drift gate must still pass.
SHALL NOT introduce any default cap, backoff, deadline, retry, or timeout — verify_fix_cap and round_backoff_ms remain the only bounds in brief_dev.
SHALL NOT keep both a workflow-side and a worker-side rendering path: prompts.gleam is deleted, not deprecated (ADR-002).
SHALL NOT lift the norn agent driver into aion_kit — it stays worker-side (ADR-011); this brief only consumes aion_kit's payload and template primitives.
SHALL NOT hand-edit src/aion_stacked_dev_io.gleam (regenerated by codegen) and SHALL NOT touch dispatch.gleam, gate.gleam, or assemble_wave (out of the thin-workflow scope).
verification: Confirm RM-022 (aion_kit) has landed and the aion_kit payload (seal/raw/peek), the aion_flow pass-through type, and the template primitive are available before dispatching this brief (blocked_by).
grep -rn 'stacked_dev/prompts' examples/stacked-dev/src examples/stacked-dev/test returns nothing, and src/stacked_dev/prompts.gleam plus the five prompts_*_test.gleam files do not exist.
grep in brief_dev.gleam for ScoutReport/DevReport/ReviewReport field access, .enrichments, and the report decoders returns nothing — the workflow reads facts only (CN2).
Diff the provision/gate-spawn/request_review/review-signal/land/startup-envelope/brief_dev-spawn blocks in stacked_dev.gleam against their pre-brief text: byte-identical (CN1).
cargo run -p aion-cli -- codegen examples/stacked-dev --check exits 0; bash examples/stacked-dev/scripts/check-schema-drift.sh exits 0 (stage contracts untouched, CN5).
cd examples/stacked-dev && gleam build && gleam test exit 0 (render_test, facts_codecs_test, and the updated hermetic suite run); cd worker && cargo test passes including wire_compat.
cargo fmt --check and cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings pass clean; no #[allow]/unwrap/expect/panic introduced; no file over 500 code lines (split render/facts if needed).
The TK-002 dogfood lands on main with the enriched brief in the merge and no workflow-process heap exhaustion, and a mid-review SIGKILL resumes with zero re-executed activities (CN9, R9).
